title: To designate the area between the intersections of 18th Street Northwest and Church Street Northwest, and 18th Street Northwest and P Street Northwest, in the District of Columbia as "Jimmy Lai Way", and for other purposes.
plain_summary: Congress wants to honor Jimmy Lai, a person from Hong Kong who fights for democracy and freedom but is now in jail. They believe he was put in prison for unfair reasons. To show support for him, Congress is naming a street in Washington DC after Jimmy Lai. The bill also tells the postal service to deliver mail addressed to this new street name to Hong Kong government offices in other US cities.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for considera

sections.title: Section 1. Condemning the Arbitrary Detention of Jimmy Lai in Hong Kong
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts about Jimmy Lai. Jimmy Lai Chee-ying is a person who supports democracy in Hong Kong. He runs media companies and is a British citizen. He has been in prison since December two thousand twenty on fake charges. He is serving a sentence of almost six years in prison for fraud charges that were made for political reasons. He is also serving two other sentences at the same time. One sentence is for joining a memorial service for people killed in the nineteen eighty nine Tiananmen Square Massacre. The other sentence is for taking part in peaceful protests in two thousand nineteen. Jimmy Lai is kept alone in his cell for twenty three hours and ten minutes each day. He only gets fifty minutes for limited exercise and has very little contact with other people or access to daylight. He has diabetes and has lost a lot of weight and become weaker since being put in prison. He was not allowed to see independent doctors. The Apple Daily newspaper that Jimmy Lai started and published was forced to close by Hong Kong authorities in June two thousand twenty one. The parent company Next Media was removed from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange because the government forced them to do it in January two thousand twenty three. This severely limited the free flow of news and information that helped Hong Kong work as a major financial center. On August tenth two thousand twenty Lai was charged with working with foreign countries or outside groups to put national security in danger under Article twenty nine of the National Security Law. These charges were made partly because of meetings he had with United States officials including those during the first presidency of Donald J Trump from two thousand sixteen to two thousand twenty. He also publicly supported the policies that administration was following at that time. Jimmy Lai's meetings with former United States government officials and public figures including the Vice President Secretary of State Speaker of the House of Representatives and Consul General in Hong Kong have been mentioned by prosecutors in court proceedings. Lai's trial under the National Security Law began in December two thousand twenty three and is still happening. A group of United Nations experts were worried about multiple and serious violations of Lai's rights including the right to a fair trial and access to a lawyer of his own choosing. They called for his immediate release. The minimum sentence required under the National Security Law is ten years in prison so it is possible that Lai could spend the rest of his life behind bars. In nineteen ninety seven the United Kingdom gave control of Hong Kong to the People's Republic of China with promises that basic rights would be protected under both the Sino British Joint Declaration and the Basic Law. These laws list specific rights and freedoms including freedom of speech freedom of the press and freedom of assembly. The Government of the People's Republic of China has repeatedly hurt Hong Kong's independence especially since the Umbrella Movement protests in two thousand fourteen the mass pro democracy protests in two thousand nineteen and when they put in place the harsh National Security Law in two thousand twenty. Since it was put in place in June two thousand twenty the National Security Law was passed by only the National People's Congress Standing Committee and enforced by officials in Hong Kong as an excuse to stop legitimate and peaceful speech assembly and expression of political opinions. Jimmy Lai represents the values of democracy human rights respect for the rule of law religious freedom freedom of expression and media freedom. He is being prosecuted and persecuted by the PRC's representatives in Hong Kong only because of his opinions and activities in pursuit of these values. Congress condemns the unfair imprisonment mistreatment and show trial of United Kingdom citizen Jimmy Lai in Hong Kong. Congress calls upon the Hong Kong authorities to release Jimmy Lai immediately and without conditions and stop prosecuting him and others charged under the National Security Law. Congress reaffirms the importance of freedom of speech and the right of publishers journalists and media workers in Hong Kong and around the world to operate free from intimidation harassment repression censorship and fear. Congress recognizes that the severe restrictions on the free flow of news and information from Hong Kong impacts the ability of individuals corporations and diplomats to make decisions about travel investments and political conditions in Hong Kong. Congress urges the Secretary of State and other relevant Department of State officials to advocate for the release of Jimmy Lai by raising his case with officials of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region and People's Republic of China. Congress urges the Secretary of State to use the financial sanctions found in the Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act and the Hong Kong Autonomy Act for Hong Kong officials who helped with the arrest and prosecution of Jimmy Lai and other political prisoners in Hong Kong. Congress urges the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights to hold a special session and appoint an impartial and independent United Nations mechanism to evaluate monitor and report on a range of human rights violations in the People's Republic of China including the impact of the National Security Law on human rights in Hong Kong as requested by over one hundred United Nations Special Experts in two thousand twenty. Congress urges the Government of the United Kingdom and all governments that view extension of human rights the rule of law and an end to censorship as critical aspects of their national and economic security to call for all charges against Jimmy Lai to be dropped the trial to end and for the immediate and unconditional release of Jimmy Lai.

sections.title: Section 2. Designation of Jimmy Lai Way in the District of Columbia
sections.text: The area between the intersections of Eighteenth Street Northwest and Church Street Northwest and Eighteenth Street Northwest and P Street Northwest in the District of Columbia will be known and designated as Jimmy Lai Way. Any reference in a law map regulation document paper or other record of the United States to the area referred to above will be considered to include Jimmy Lai Way. The address of fifteen twenty Eighteenth Street Northwest in the District of Columbia will be changed to One Jimmy Lai Way Northwest. Any reference in a law map regulation document paper or other record of the United States to the address referred to above will be considered to be a reference to One Jimmy Lai Way Northwest. The Administrator of General Services will build street signs that contain the phrase Jimmy Lai Way NW. These signs will be similar in design to the signs used by the District of Columbia to show the location of Metro stations. The signs will be placed on the piece of Federal property that is closest to One Jimmy Lai Way Northwest in the District of Columbia and on the street corners of Eighteenth Street Northwest and Church Street Northwest and Eighteenth Street Northwest and P Street Northwest in the District of Columbia.

sections.title: Section 3. Sense of Congress Regarding Mail Delivery by the United States Postal Service to 1 Jimmy Lai Way
sections.text: It is the sense of Congress that the Postmaster General should recognize any mail which is addressed to the Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office at Jimmy Lai Way in San Francisco California or New York New York with the appropriate postal codes. The Postmaster General should deliver such mail to One hundred thirty Montgomery Street San Francisco California nine four one zero four or One hundred fifteen East Fifty fourth Street New York New York one zero zero two two as the case may be.
